span.p: Timing gap, or structural — burn exceeds inflow ongoing?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Not a treasury-lever problem alone
→ FP&A: cut burn / re-plan; escalate (raise or restructure)

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
1. Accelerate receivables (collections, deposits, factoring)
2. Stretch payables within terms
3. Defer discretionary spend

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Draw the committed revolver
(cheapest external cash; check covenant impact of the draw)

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Monetize: AR financing, asset sale-leaseback,
delay non-critical capex

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
New financing — debt before equity if capacity exists
(financing tree); equity / emergency last
